results.phrase: We don't have time for a full routine. Let's figure out what we can't walk out without.
results.usage: Use this when a deadline is imminent and the team needs to immediately triage what is essential versus what has to be cut.
results.topic: we are three sprints behind and the big stakeholder review is tomorrow morning

results.phrase: We need to tweeze, not wax. Let's agree on what we're actually removing before we take off too much.
results.usage: Use this when a team needs to make precise, deliberate cuts rather than letting someone make a broad call that can't be undone.
results.topic: we have to cut features before launch but we can't agree on which ones to cut

results.phrase: We've been buffing the same spot for three weeks. At some point you just have to set it and move on.
results.usage: Use this when a decision has been over-deliberated and the team needs permission to stop refining and commit.
results.topic: we've been debating the same decision for three weeks and nothing is moving

results.phrase: This isn't ready to leave the house. We need to talk about the timeline.
results.usage: Use this when a launch date is approaching but the product has too many unresolved issues to ship with confidence.
results.topic: we're supposed to launch next week but the product still has too many rough edges
results.note: Things about house, bag, chair etc. really don’t work. They don’t make any sense. This is still too random and cringe

results.phrase: We've been on our feet since open. The team needs a sit-down before we lose them.
results.usage: Use this when sustained pressure has depleted the team and leadership needs to acknowledge it before people start checking out.
results.topic: the team has been in crunch mode for two months and morale is tanking

results.phrase: I know we wanted the full glam team. We're going to have to do it ourselves.
results.usage: Use this when you need to tell your team that requested resources were denied and they will have to absorb the work with what they have.
results.topic: I have to tell my team we're not getting the additional headcount we asked for
results.note: Don’t use random structural things like team, crew, bag, chair it more about technique, products, brands.

results.phrase: We started with a base coat and now we're doing a full nail art set — on the same time and budget.
results.usage: Use this when a client keeps expanding the scope of a project without acknowledging that additional work requires additional resources.
results.topic: the client keeps adding requirements without adjusting the timeline or budget
results.note: The vibe is fine but they are long and unseemly and really cringe. Like why would u say this vs what is normal talk. The point is that it’s catchy and can actually replace metaphors.

results.phrase: We've been doing no-makeup makeup. It's time to actually go bold.
results.usage: Use this when stakeholders are pushing for more ambitious moves after the team has been playing it safe.
results.topic: we got feedback that our roadmap is too conservative and we need to take bigger swings
results.note: Alternate: They don’t like the soft-glam, they want a full beat

results.phrase: We can't keep changing the part mid-blowout.
results.usage: Use this when leadership is redirecting the team before they have had a chance to finish what was already in motion.
results.topic: leadership keeps changing the priorities mid-sprint and the team is frustrated
results.note: I like the vibe but part?? Who will get it??

results.phrase: Two lead artists both grabbing for the same brush isn't a creative process. It's just a stalled chair.
results.usage: Use this when a standoff between two senior people has become the blocker and someone needs to name it directly.
results.topic: two senior engineers fundamentally disagree on the technical approach and it's blocking the whole team
results.note: This has to be the worst.

results.phrase: We've been on set since 6 a.m. The team needs a touch-up break before the next look.
results.usage: Use this when sustained pressure has visibly worn people down and a deliberate reset is needed before the next push.
results.topic: the team has been in crunch mode for two months and morale is tanking
